proofClass: symbolic identity with interval/ball certificate inputs
statement: The augmented repair forms are compatible with the finite null fibers and hence with the transported trace quotient relation.

finiteMuNullFiberInputStatus.label: finite Mu null-fiber input
finiteMuNullFiberInputStatus.reason: The finite Schur consequence proves Mu annihilates ker R_aug,N.

finiteSchurRangeInputStatus.label: finite Schur range input
finiteSchurRangeInputStatus.reason: The finite Schur range condition makes the repair depend only on augmented trace coordinates.

traceQuotientCompatibilityInputStatus.label: trace quotient compatibility input
traceQuotientCompatibilityInputStatus.reason: The quotient compatibility theorem identifies finite trace equivalence with X_aug.

repairNullFiberCompatibilityStatus.label: repair null-fiber compatibility
repairNullFiberCompatibilityStatus.reason: On the finite cores the repair vanishes on trace-null directions; quotient compatibility transports the same representative independence to X_aug.

proof: Import finite Mu annihilation and Schur range compatibility.
Those finite identities say the repair value is unchanged by adding a vector in ker R_aug,N.
Use trace quotient compatibility to pass representative independence to the transported trace quotient.
